summary: Recent updates to the testing framework in Firefox have focused on improving the stability and reliability of media-related tests. One key change is the fix for shutdown crashes that were occurring during media shutdown tests, which ensures that these tests run smoothly without unexpected interruptions. Additionally, the serialization between MFShutdown (for media framework shutdown) and MFTEnumEx (for enumerating media transforms) helps to prevent deadlocks, further enhancing test reliability. The team also expanded the timeout for launching the GStreamer Media Pipeline (GMP) tests on Windows, allowing for more thorough testing and re-enabling previously skipped tests. Finally, a new check was implemented to ensure that the handling of HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) is correctly mapped when extensions are lacking, reinforcing the support for this important video format. **Overall, these improvements are vital for ensuring media features in Firefox work seamlessly and without crashing, ultimately benefiting users who rely on media playback in their web experience.**
